title: Day 1
content: Today was the first day of beginning to work on the Tiny Homes. Alexander Dawson students Birdie Cardinal, Hannah Johnson, Asher Greer, Jack Kennedy, and Charles Lynch cleared the barn in preparation for setting up the workshop. The barn was filled with junk and had a pungent oder. Filled with toxic waste, trash, and leftover building materials the work was unglamorous and hard. Some of the tasks to be completed included clearing out an outside staging area for the lumber, cleaning the junk inside of the barn and delivering it to the dump, and removing a lot of toxic waste. While these are just the first steps, this will be crucial for the project. This barn will act as the main workshop where the walls for the house will be framed. The work was exhausting and left us dirty and tired but it felt good to take the first step towards building a home.
excerpt: The first day was focused on clearing out the barn to act as a workshop for the project.
author: Tipi Raisers Team

title: Day 3
content: On the third day of work, we made lots of progress on preparing the workshop for building. Some students from Dawson School, Birdie Cardinal, Hannah Johnson, Charles Lynch, and Audrey Moulton got to work preparing tools in the area. These students set up an organization system for our tools, screwing in pegboards, hanging hammers, and making sure we had a clear area to store our instruments. Other students today focused on making our salvaged wood ready to use.The planks were filled with various types of nails from the previous building project, so  Ruby Gutman, Asher Greer, and Evie Zaug worked on pulling these nails out of the planks. One vital piece of the workspace we were missing was a workbench, so two students, Noah Orr and Reid Eisenhard, decided to build one. This workbench will greatly help us during the project, and was a great opportunity for students to practice the critical skill of carpentry that will be essential to the construction process of the house. This day was very productive, and got us all prepared to learn how to start building the house.
excerpt: On the third day, students organized tools, prepared salvaged wood, and built a workbench to get the workshop ready for construction.
author: Tipi Raisers Team

title: Day 4
content: The fourth day of work was a day of learning. This day was a training process in using power tools that are necessary for the construction, but simultaneously a hazard. To avoid as much danger as possible, we invited everyone to the hub for a masterclass from (insert name I forgot), an employee at a local tool library. We learned about the various tools we would be working with, from miter saws & jigsaws, to nail guns and power drills. The students from Dawson and adults on the project learned the basics and safety of these machines, and then got a chance to practice using each and every tool. Everyone did an amazing job today, (no fingers were lost), and we all feel substantially more prepared to build the house safely and skillfully.
excerpt: On the fourth day, students received hands-on safety training with power tools like saws, drills, and nail guns, helping everyone gain confidence and prepare for safe construction work.
author: Tipi Raisers Team
